steps.text: Provide training to each employee who is required by this section to use PPE.
Train each employee to know when PPE is necessary.
Train each employee to know what PPE is necessary.
Train each employee on how to properly don, doff, adjust and wear PPE.
Train each employee about the limitations of the PPE.
Train each employee on the proper care, maintenance, useful life, and disposal of the PPE.
Ensure each affected employee demonstrates an understanding of the training and the ability to use PPE properly before performing work requiring the use of PPE.
Retrain each affected employee if there is reason to believe they do not have the required understanding and skill.

constraints.text: Training must be comprehensive, understandable, and recur annually, and more often if necessary.

steps.text: Evaluate a prospective rescuer’s ability to respond to a rescue summons in a timely manner, considering the hazard(s) identified.

Evaluate a prospective rescue service’s ability, in terms of proficiency with rescue-related tasks and equipment, to function appropriately while rescuing entrants from the particular permit space or types of permit spaces identified.
Select a rescue team or service from those evaluated that has the capability to reach the victim(s) within a time frame that is appropriate for the permit space hazard(s) identified.
Select a rescue team or service from those evaluated that is equipped for and proficient in performing the needed rescue services.
Inform each rescue team or service of the hazards they may confront when called on to perform rescue at the site.
Develop, document and utilize procedures for the control of potentially hazardous energy when employees are engaged in the activities covered by this section.
Receive a training and indoctrination program prior to being authorized to enter a regulated area.
Understand the nature of the carcinogenic hazards including local and systemic toxicity.
Learn the specific nature of operations involving a carcinogen that could result in exposure.
Understand the purpose for and application of the medical surveillance program, including methods of self-examination.
Learn the purpose for and application of decontamination practices.
Understand the purpose for and significance of emergency practices and procedures.
Understand the employee’s specific role in emergency procedures.
Learn specific information to recognize and evaluate conditions that may result in the release of a carcinogen.
Understand the purpose for and application of specific first-aid procedures and practices.
Review this section at the employee’s first training and indoctrination program and annually thereafter.
Familiarize with and rehearse specific emergency procedures that shall be prescribed and posted.
Receive training in a program relating to the hazards of vinyl chloride and precautions for its safe use.
Train each employee who may be exposed to DBCP in accordance with the requirements of this section.
Institute a training program and ensure employee participation in the program.
Inform each employee of the information contained in Appendix A.

Inform each employee of the quantity, location, manner of use, release or storage of DBCP and the specific nature of operations which could result in exposure to DBCP as well as any necessary protective steps.
Inform each employee of the purpose, proper use, and limitations of respirators.
Inform each employee of the purpose and description of the medical surveillance program required by paragraph (m) of this section.
Inform each employee of a review of this standard, including appendices.
Make a copy of this standard and its appendices readily available to all affected employees.
Provide, upon request, all materials relating to the employee information and training program to the Assistant Secretary and the Director.
Inform all affected employees of the location of written training materials and make these materials readily available, without cost, to the affected employees.
Provide, upon request, all training materials relating to the employee training program to the Assistant Secretary and the Director.
Provide employees with information and training on MDA in accordance with 29 CFR 1910.1200(h) at the time of initial assignment and at least annually thereafter.
Provide an explanation of the contents of this section, including Appendices A and B, and indicate to employees where a copy of the standard is available.
Describe the medical surveillance program required under paragraph (m) of this section, and explain the information contained in Appendix C.
Describe the medical removal provision required under paragraph (m) of this section.
Make readily available to all affected employees, without cost, all written materials relating to the employee training program, including a copy of this regulation.
Provide to the Assistant Secretary of Labor and the Director, upon request, all information and training materials relating to the employee information and training program.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to recognize the characteristics of the confined space.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to anticipate and be aware of the hazards that may be faced during entry.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to recognize the adverse health effects that may be caused by the exposure to a hazard.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to understand the physical signs and reactions related to exposures to such hazards.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to know what personal protective equipment is needed for safe entry into and exit from the space.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to use personal protective equipment.
Ensure that each employee who enters a confined space is trained to be aware of the presence and proper use of barriers that may be needed to protect an entrant from hazards.
Ensure that each entrant into confined or enclosed spaces is trained to exit the space or dangerous atmosphere whenever the employer or a representative orders evacuation.
Ensure that each entrant into confined or enclosed spaces is trained to exit the space or dangerous atmosphere whenever an evacuation signal such as an alarm is activated.
Ensure that each entrant into confined or enclosed spaces is trained to exit the space or dangerous atmosphere whenever the entrant perceives that he or she is in danger.
Provide each employee with training before the entrant begins work addressed by this section.
Provide each employee with training whenever there is a change in operations or in an employee’s duties that present a hazard about which the employee has not previously been trained.
Certify that the training required has been accomplished.
Ensure the certification contains the employee’s name, the name of the certifier, and the date(s) of the certification.
Make the certification available for inspection by the Assistant Secretary, the Director, employees, and their representatives.
Make all repairs to chains under qualified supervision.
Replace defective links in the chain with links having proper dimensions and made of similar material.
Proof test repaired chains to the proof test load recommended by the manufacturer before returning them to service.
Assign an individual familiar with the signal code to act as a signalman when the hoist operator cannot see the load being handled.
Ensure clear and distinct visual or auditory signals are used for communication; verbal signals shall not be permitted.
Determine that the ship's gear is properly rigged, in safe condition, and will not be overloaded by the size and weight of the lift before hoisting materials aboard.
Ensure only employees who understand signs, notices, and operating instructions, and are familiar with the signal code, are permitted to operate a crane, winch, or other hoisting apparatus.
Instruct employees on the safe use of powder actuated tools before they are permitted to use them.
Inspect powder actuated tools to ensure they are clean, moving parts operate freely, and the barrel is free from obstructions before use.
Ensure employees have access to labels on containers of lead and safety data sheets.
Train each employee who is subject to exposure to lead at or above the action level.
Institute a training program and ensure employee participation in the program.
Provide the training program as initial training prior to the time of job assignment or prior to the start-up date, whichever comes last.
Provide the training program at least annually for each employee who is subject to lead exposure at or above the action level.

constraints.text: Ensure that the following hazards are addressed: reproductive/developmental toxicity, central nervous system effects, kidney effects, blood effects, and acute toxicity effects.

steps.text: Select a trained individual or assemble a trained team of people to audit the process safety management system and program.
Evaluate the design and effectiveness of the process safety management system and conduct a field inspection.
Plan the audit program.
Staff the audit team.
Conduct the audit.
Evaluate and implement corrective actions.
Follow up on the audit findings.
Document the audit process.
Review the relevant documentation and process safety information.
Inspect the physical facilities.
Interview all levels of plant personnel.
Review the written training program for adequacy of content, frequency, and effectiveness.
Determine employee knowledge and awareness of safety procedures.
Observe actual practices related to safety and health policies.
Identify deficiencies and determine corrective actions or improvements.
Complete training for emergency response employees before they perform in real emergencies.
Ensure training includes elements of the emergency response plan and standard operating procedures.
Train employee members of TSD facility emergency response organizations to a level of competence in recognition of health and safety hazards.
Certify that each covered employee has attended and successfully completed the required training.
Rehearse the emergency response plan regularly as part of the overall training program.

preconditions.text: If the employer divides the work force such that a sufficient number of employees have the training specified, then not all employees need to be trained to the degree specified.
If arrangements have been made for an outside fully-trained emergency response team, then not all employees need to be trained to the degree specified.

constraints.text: Personal protective equipment must be worn during emergency response training.
Employees must be trained in safe operating procedures to be used at the incident scene.

steps.text: Ensure each employee can demonstrate knowledge of the necessity of the respirator and the effects of improper fit, usage, or maintenance.
Ensure each employee knows the limitations and capabilities of the respirator.
Ensure each employee knows how to use the respirator effectively in emergency situations.
Ensure each employee knows how to inspect, put on and remove, use, and check the seals of the respirator.
Ensure each employee knows the procedures for maintenance and storage of the respirator.
Ensure each employee can recognize medical signs and symptoms that may limit or prevent effective use of respirators.
Conduct training in a manner that is understandable to the employee.
Provide training prior to requiring the employee to use a respirator in the workplace.
Administer retraining annually and when specific situations occur.

preconditions.text: A new employee must demonstrate knowledge of the training elements prior to using a respirator if training was received within the last 12 months.
Retraining is necessary if changes in the workplace or type of respirator render previous training obsolete.
Retraining is necessary if inadequacies in the employee's knowledge or use of the respirator indicate that they have not retained the requisite understanding or skill.
Retraining is necessary if any other situation arises in which retraining appears necessary to ensure safe respirator use.

steps.text: Instruct every employee going under air pressure for the first time on how to avoid excessive discomfort.
Qualify a blaster by reason of training, knowledge, or experience in transporting, storing, handling, and use of explosives.
Instruct and train employees in the live-line bare-hand technique and the safety requirements pertinent thereto before permitting them to use the technique on energized circuits.
Ensure all live-line bare-hand work is personally supervised by a person trained and qualified to perform live-line bare-hand work.

Provide a training program for each employee using ladders and stairways to recognize hazards related to ladders and stairways and train them in the procedures to be followed to minimize these hazards.
Include cadmium in the Hazard Communication program.
Ensure that each employee has access to labels on containers of cadmium.
Ensure that each employee has access to safety data sheets.
Train employees in accordance with the provisions of HCS.
Provide information on hazards including cancer, lung effects, kidney effects, and acute toxicity effects.
